remote_metadata.name: New York City Bike Routes
remote_metadata.description: The New York City Department of Transportation (NYC DOT) builds and manages bicycle facilities across the five boroughs, alongside other City agencies, New York State, and external partners. This dataset contain records of the current and historic network of designated bicycle routes and facilities, including bicycle facility type and relevant street information represented as line segments. Additional information about NYC DOT's commitment to safe all-ages and abilities bicycling, along with data about the growth of bicycling in NYC and PDF maps of the current network can be found at: https://www.nyc.gov/html/dot/html/bicyclists/bicyclists.shtml

columns.name: the_geom
columns.description: Geometry column used for mapping

columns.name: segmentid
columns.description: From LION 23C. A number of up to seven digits that identifies each segment of a street or a non-street feature represented in the LION file. Nonstandard Segment IDs reflect a difference in geometry from LION 23C and existing conditions.

columns.name: bikeid
columns.description: A unique number that corresponds to one or more LION segments that share all other attributes.

columns.name: prevbikeid
columns.description: A unique number that identifies the related record of a Bike ID that has been superseded.

columns.name: status
columns.description: Indicates whether a record represents a current (i.e. existing) bicycle facility or a retired (i.e. historic) one.

columns.name: boro
columns.description: A 1-digit code identifying the borough in which the left side of the street segment is located.

columns.name: street
columns.description: Name of the street or facility.

columns.name: fromstreet
columns.description: The street, when available, or where no street is present a park, waterfront line, land mark, or nearest street adjacent to the beginning of the facility route.

columns.name: tostreet
columns.description: The street, when available, or where no street is present a park, waterfront line, land mark, or nearest street adjacent to the end of the facility route.

columns.name: onoffst
columns.description: Indicates whether a bicycle facility is on-street or off-street.

columns.name: facilitycl
columns.description: The HIGHEST facility class found along a segment.

columns.name: allclasses
columns.description: Lists ALL bicycle facility classes (separated by commas) that are present on a given segment.

columns.name: bikedir
columns.description: Travel direction based on LION geometry.

columns.name: lanecount
columns.description: The number of bike lanes on a segment in any direction.

columns.name: ft_facilit
columns.description: From-To Facility. Type of bicycle facility in the direction of the STARTpoint TO ENDpoint of the segment.

columns.name: tf_facilit
columns.description: To-From Facility. Type of bicycle facility in the direction of the ENDpoint TO STARTpoint of the segment.

columns.name: ft2facilit
columns.description: From-To Facility 2. (On segments with lane counts of 3 or greater, or multiple parallel bicycle facilities) Type of bicycle facility in the direction of the STARTpoint TO ENDpoint of the segment.

columns.name: tf2facilit
columns.description: To-From Facility 2. (On segments with lane counts of 3 or greater, or multiple parallel bicycle facilities) Type of bicycle facility in the direction of the ENDpoint TO STARTpoint of the segment.

columns.name: instdate
columns.description: Install Date. The approximate or exact date when the installation or modification of a bicycle facility was completed.

columns.name: ret_date
columns.description: Retired Date. The date when a BIKE ID or a bike facility was removed or superseded by a new facility or Bike ID.

columns.name: grnwy
columns.description: Greenway. Indicates the presence of a greenway alignment.

columns.name: gwsystem
columns.description: Greenway System Name

columns.name: gwsys2
columns.description: Secondary Greenway System Name, if an alignment includes two greenway systems.

columns.name: spur
columns.description: Greenway Spur Name, if the bikeway includes a secondary alignment.

columns.name: gwyjuris
columns.description: Greenway Jurisdiction

metadata.rowLabel: Record of a bicycle facility on a LION segment. Each row may represent a new installation or a modification of a previously existing bicycle facility.

remote_metadata.name: Active NYC Health Code Regulated Child Care Programs
remote_metadata.attribution: Department of health and Mental Hygiene
remote_metadata.description:
This is an official NYC Health Department dataset. the Health Department does not endorse datasets using this data that may be created by others.

This dataset lists active child care programs regulated under NYC Health Code Article 47 (Group Day Care) and Article 43 (School-based Child Care). 

Health Code Article 47: https://www.nyc.gov/assets/doh/downloads/pdf/about/healthcode/health-code-article47.pdf

Health Code Article 43: https://www.nyc.gov/assets/doh/downloads/pdf/about/healthcode/health-code-article43.pdf

These data can be used to map NYC Health Code regulated child care programs and filter by attributes, such as location, ages served, facility type. It can also be used to answer questions such as how many group child care (GCC) programs are in a specific ZIP code.

Only active group child care (GCC) and school-based child care (SBCC) programs are displayed in this dataset. Child care programs with a 'preliminary', 'suspended', or 'closed' status are not included.

columns.description: Unique Identifier for the child Care programs

columns.name: Permit Number
columns.description: Permit number for a group child care program

columns.name: Program Name

remote_metadata.name: 311 Service Requests from 2010 to 2019
remote_metadata.description:
311 responds to thousands of inquiries, comments and requests from customers every single day. This dataset represents only service requests that can be directed to specific agencies.  

This dataset is updated daily and expected values for many fields will change over time.  The lists of expected values associated with each column are not exhaustive.

Each row of data contains information about the service request, including complaint type, responding agency, and geographic location.  However the data does not reveal any personally identifying information about the customer who made the request.

For data from 2020-present see: https://data.cityofnewyork.us/Social-Services/311-Service-Requests-from-2020-to-Present/erm2-nwe9/about_data

columns.name: Unique Key
columns.description: Unique identifier of a Service Request (SR) in the open data set.

columns.name: Created Date
columns.description: The date and time that a Customer submits a Service Request.

columns.name: Closed Date
columns.description: The date and time that an Agency closes a Service Request.

columns.name: Agency
columns.description: Acronym of responding City Government Agency or entity responding to 311 Service Request.

columns.name: Agency Name
columns.description: Full agency name of responding City Government Agency, or entity responding to 311 service request.

columns.name: Additional Details

remote_metadata.name: Bi-Annual Pedestrian Counts
remote_metadata.description:
An index of pedestrian volumes tracking the long-term trends of neighborhood commercial corridors. Data is collected at 114 locations, including 100 on-street locations (primarily retail corridors), 13 East River and Harlem River bridge locations, and the Hudson River Greenway. Screenline sampling is conducted during May and September on the sidewalk, mid-block (or mid-bridge) on both sides of street where applicable. Pedestrian volumes at 50 sample locations around the City are combined to create the Pedestrian Volume Index for the Mayor’s Management Report.


Click here for metadata - http://www.nyc.gov/html/dot/downloads/pdf/bi-annual-ped-count-readme.pdf

columns.name: Oct24_AM
columns.description: October 2024 Counts

columns.name: Oct24_PM
columns.description: October 2024 PM Counts

columns.name: Oct24_MD
columns.description: October 2024 Midday Counts

remote_metadata.name: Schoolyard to Playgrounds
remote_metadata.description:
The Schoolyards to Playgrounds initiative began with PlaNYC 2007 as a way to expand public use at existing City facilities and improve access to open spaces during non-school hours which includes weekends, holidays, and during the summer. 
 
NYC Parks, in collaboration with the Department of Education (DOE) and the nonprofit Trust for Public Land have improved hundreds of DOE operated schoolyards through capital improvements, including play equipment, painted sports surfaces, trees, and benches.
 
The program is an important approach to reaching the city’s target to getting more New Yorkers within walking distance to open-space. In many neighborhoods, these DOE schoolyards are one of the few resources that can be improved and provide publicly accessible open space.
 
Each record in this dataset represents a participating DOE Schoolyard at the time of the dataset's publication. The schoolyard's boundaries have been drawn in whole or part using secondary data. Data accuracy is limited by the scale and accuracy of the original sources. Site-specific conditions should be field-verified. 
 
 For more information on the Schoolyard to Playgrounds program, visit: https://www.nycgovparks.org/greening/planyc/schoolyards
 
This dataset may be updated throughout the year as school's join or exit the program.

Data Dictionary: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/10yVRhmdIAS1g8c3L0jC4XxSTptdIf5wESL5ks1e6Gto/edit?usp=sharing

remote_metadata.name: 2015 Street Tree Census - Tree Data
remote_metadata.description:
Street tree data from the TreesCount! 2015 Street Tree Census, conducted by volunteers and staff organized by NYC Parks & Recreation and partner organizations. Tree data collected includes tree species, diameter and perception of health. Accompanying blockface data is available indicating status of data collection and data release citywide.

The 2015 tree census was the third decadal street tree census and largest citizen science initiative in NYC Parks’ history. Data collection ran from May 2015 to October 2016 and the results of the census show that there are 666,134 trees planted along NYC's streets. The data collected as part of the census represents a snapshot in time of trees under NYC Parks' jurisdiction.

The census data formed the basis of our operational database, the Forestry Management System (ForMS) which is used daily by our foresters and other staff for inventory and asset management: https://data.cityofnewyork.us/browse?sortBy=most_accessed&utf8=%E2%9C%93&Data-Collection_Data-Collection=Forestry+Management+System+%28ForMS%29

To learn more about the data collected and managed in ForMS, please refer to this user guide:
https://docs.google.com/document/d/1PVPWFi-WExkG3rvnagQDoBbqfsGzxCKNmR6n678nUeU/edit. For information on the city's current tree population, use the ForMS datasets.

columns.name: tree_id
columns.description: Unique identification number for each tree point.

columns.name: block_id
columns.description: Identifier linking each tree to the block in the blockface table/shapefile that it is mapped on.

columns.name: created_at
columns.description: The date tree points were collected in the census software.

columns.name: tree_dbh
columns.description: Diameter of the tree, measured at approximately 54" / 137cm above the ground. Data was collected for both living and dead trees; for stumps, use stump_diam

columns.name: stump_diam
columns.description: Diameter of stump measured through the center, rounded to the nearest inch.

columns.name: curb_loc
columns.description: Location of tree bed in relationship to the curb; trees are either along the curb (OnCurb) or offset from the curb (OffsetFromCurb)

columns.name: status
columns.description: Indicates whether the tree is alive, standing dead, or a stump.

columns.name: health
columns.description: Indicates the user's perception of tree health.

columns.name: spc_latin
columns.description: Scientific name for species, e.g. "Acer rubrum"

columns.name: spc_common
columns.description: Common name for species, e.g. "red maple"

columns.name: steward
columns.description: Indicates the number of unique signs of stewardship observed for this tree. Not recorded for stumps or dead trees.

columns.name: guards
columns.description: Indicates whether a guard is present, and if the user felt it was a helpful or harmful guard. Not recorded for dead trees and stumps.

columns.name: sidewalk
columns.description: Indicates whether one of the sidewalk flags immediately adjacent to the tree was damaged, cracked, or lifted. Not recorded for dead trees and stumps.

columns.name: user_type
columns.description: This field describes the category of user who collected this tree point's data.

columns.name: root_stone
columns.description: Indicates the presence of a root problem caused by paving stones in tree bed

columns.name: root_grate
columns.description: Indicates the presence of a root problem caused by metal grates in tree bed

columns.name: root_other
columns.description: Indicates the presence of other root problems

columns.name: trunk_wire
columns.description: Indicates the presence of a trunk problem caused by wires or rope wrapped around the trunk

columns.name: trnk_light
columns.description: Indicates the presence of a trunk problem caused by lighting installed on the tree

columns.name: trnk_other
columns.description: Indicates the presence of other trunk problems

columns.name: brch_light
columns.description: Indicates the presence of a branch problem caused by lights (usually string lights) or wires in the branches

columns.name: brch_shoe
columns.description: Indicates the presence of a branch problem caused by sneakers in the branches

columns.name: brch_other
columns.description: Indicates the presence of other branch problems

columns.name: address
columns.description: Nearest estimated address to tree

columns.name: postcode
columns.description: Five-digit zipcode in which tree is located

columns.name: zip_city
columns.description: City as derived from zipcode. This is often (but not always) the same as borough.

columns.name: community board
columns.description: Community board in which tree point is located

columns.name: borocode
columns.description:
Code for borough in which tree point is located:
1 (Manhattan), 2 (Bronx), 3 (Brooklyn), 4 (Queens), 5 (Staten Island)

columns.name: borough
columns.description: Name of borough in which tree point is located

columns.name: cncldist
columns.description: Council district in which tree point is located

columns.name: st_assem
columns.description: State Assembly District in which tree point is located

columns.name: st_senate
columns.description: State Senate District in which tree point is located

columns.name: nta
columns.description: This is the NTA Code corresponding to the neighborhood tabulation area from the 2010 US Census that the tree point falls into.

columns.name: nta_name
columns.description: This is the NTA name corresponding to the neighborhood tabulation area from the 2010 US Census that the tree point falls into.

columns.name: boro_ct
columns.description: This is the boro_ct identifyer for the census tract that the tree point falls into.

columns.name: state
columns.description: All features given value 'New York'

columns.name: latitude
columns.description: Latitude of point, in decimal degrees

columns.name: longitude
columns.description: Longitude of point, in decimal degrees

columns.name: x_sp
columns.description: X coordinate, in state plane. Units are feet.

columns.name: y_sp
columns.description: Y coordinate, in state plane. Units are feet

remote_metadata.name: NYC Wi-Fi Hotspot Locations
remote_metadata.description:
NYC Wi-Fi Hotspot Locations Wi-Fi Providers: 
CityBridge, LLC (Free Beta): LinkNYC 1 gigabyte (GB), Free Wi-Fi Internet Kiosks
Spot On Networks (Free) NYC HOUSING AUTHORITY (NYCHA) Properties
Fiberless (Free): Wi-Fi access on Governors Island Free - up to 5 Mbps for users as the part of Governors Island Trust Governors Island Connectivity Challenge
AT&T (Free): Wi-Fi access is free for all users at all times. 
Partners: In several parks, the NYC partner organizations provide publicly accessible Wi-Fi. Visit these parks to learn more information about their Wi-Fi service and how to connect. 
Cable (Limited-Free): In NYC Parks provided by NYC DoITT Cable television franchisees. 
ALTICEUSA previously known as “Cablevision” and SPECTRUM previously known as “Time Warner Cable” (Limited Free) Connect for 3 free 10 minute sessions every 30 days or purchase a 99 cent day pass through midnight. Wi-Fi service is free at all times to Cablevision’s Optimum Online and Time Warner Cable broadband subscribers. 
Wi-Fi Provider: Chelsea Wi-Fi (Free) Wi-Fi access is free for all users at all times. 
Chelsea Improvement Company has partnered with Google to provide Wi-Fi a free wireless Internet zone, a broadband region bounded by West 19th Street, Gansevoort Street, Eighth Avenue, and the High Line Park. 
Wi-Fi Provider: Downtown Brooklyn Wi-Fi (Free) 
The Downtown Brooklyn Partnership - the New York City Economic Development Corporation to provide Wi-Fi to the area bordered by Schermerhorn Street, Cadman Plaza West, Flatbush Avenue, and Tillary Street, along with select public spaces in the NYCHA Ingersoll and Whitman Houses. 
Wi-Fi Provider: Manhattan Downtown Alliance Wi-Fi (Free) 
Lower Manhattan Several public spaces all along Water Street, Front Street and the East River Esplanade south of Fulton Street and in several other locations throughout Lower Manhattan. 
Wi-Fi Provider: Harlem Wi-Fi (Free) 
The network will extend 95 city blocks, from 110th to 138th Streets between Frederick Douglass Boulevard and Madison Avenue is the free outdoor public wireless network. 
Wi-Fi Provider: Transit Wireless (Free) 
Wi-Fi Services in the New York City subway system is available in certain underground stations. For more information visit http://www.transitwireless.com/stations/. 
Wi-Fi Provider: Public Pay Telephone Franchisees (Free) 
Using existing payphone infrastructure, the City of New York has teamed up with private partners to provide free Wi-Fi service at public payphone kiosks across the five boroughs at no cost to taxpayers. 
Wi-Fi Provider: New York Public Library 
Using Wireless Internet Access (Wi-Fi): All Library locations offer free wireless access (Wi-Fi) in public areas at all times the libraries are open. 
Connecting to the Library's Wireless Network 
•You must have a computer or other device equipped with an 802.11b-compatible wireless card. 
•Using your computer's network utilities, look for the wireless network named "NYPL." 
•The "NYPL" wireless network does not require a password to connect. 
Limitations and Disclaimers Regarding Wireless Access 
•The Library's wireless network is not secure. Information sent from or to your laptop can be captured by anyone else with a wireless device and the appropriate software, within three hundred feet. 
•Library staff is not able to provide technical assistance and no guarantee can be provided that you will be able to make a wireless connection. 
•The Library assumes no responsibility for the safety of equipment or for laptop configurations, security, or data files resulting from connection to the Library's network
